mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 7.0% signals labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings revisions, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in >60% of instances following a UNRATE drop below 7.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth, which directly increases corporate revenue forecasts and pushes S&P 500 returns higher over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive rank IC over 60-day forward returns in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output that reduces corporate profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in late-cycle periods, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in regime-filtered samples.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day SPX direction with an accuracy exceeding 55% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of periods following CPIAUCSL monthly increases above 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, which raises equity valuations through improved earnings outlooks over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decrease yields a negative or zero rank IC for 20-day forward returns across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in nonfarm payrolls during a post-shock recovery phase indicate rapid labor market normalization that reduces the marginal impact of fiscal stimulus, leading to negative short-term equity returns as markets price in policy withdrawal.
failure_condition: PAYEMS spikes in recovery regimes do not precede negative 5-day SPX returns, or the signal accuracy drops below 45% in regime-specific backtests.
